source.title: White Paper: Description of the Canon Log, Canon Log 2 and Canon Log 3 Gamma Curves
source.extraction_note: Formulas and the code-value table are embedded as images in the PDF (no text layer); extracted by direct visual reading of pages 8 to 9 rendered at 150dpi, then every value cross-checked against colour-science 0.4.7 log_decoding_CanonLog3 before acceptance. All checks passed within table rounding.

notes: Canon Log 3 (Full %) encode, x = Scene Linear %, y = Full Range %: x < -0.014: y = -0.36726845*log10(1 - 14.98325x) + 0.12783901; -0.014 <= x <= 0.014: y = 1.9754798x + 0.12512219; x > 0.014: y = 0.36726845*log10(14.98325x + 1) + 0.12240537. Inverse thresholds: y < 0.097465473 (negative branch), 0.097465473 <= y <= 0.15277891 (linear), y > 0.15277891 (log branch). CONVENTION (document footnote, page 9): reflection = Scene Linear * 0.9, so the table's 20% scene-linear grey is 0.18 reflection; colour-science operates in reflection domain by default and agrees with every table row once the 0.9 factor is applied (verified 2026-07-19: decode(0.343)=0.1794, decode(0.564)=0.8972, decode(0.887)=7.192).

vectors.name: black0
vectors.quote: Canon Log 3 row: 0% (0% Black) Full % 12.5%, 10bit CV 128

vectors.name: grey18
vectors.quote: Canon Log 3 row: 20% (18% Grey) Full % 34.3%, 10bit CV 351; reflection = Scene Linear * 0.9

vectors.name: white90
vectors.quote: Canon Log 3 row: 100% (90% White) Full % 56.4%, 10bit CV 577
